titlestmt.titleproper: Marketing plan for the "All Eyes On Us" tour8163

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-01-09 00:00:20 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Marketing plan for the "All Eyes On Us" tour

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: 15 unbound computer generated leaves. Includes mockups or designs for flyers, billboards, and advertising for the sides of buses, also with a small promotional palm card for the tour.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The C&J Concert Promotions Marketing Plan for the Aborted "All Eyes On Us" tour was prepared by Calvin Carothers. The 38-date tour, which was scheduled to begin in October 1996, never happened as the result of Tupac's murder in September 1996. After Tupac's death, the concert tour was rescheduled to proceed with just Snoop Dog but was first postponed (prompting the curious headline in the Greensboro News and Record: "Slaying Prompts Concert Delay"), and eventually canceled outright out of respect because of the murder of Biggie Smalls, and Snoop Dog's concern for his own safety.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Marketing plan for the "All Eyes On Us" tour, #8163. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
